## 9.2 Getting Started


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pacman::p_load(ggdist, ggridges, ggthemes,
               colorspace, tidyverse)
```
:::


## 9.2.2 Data import


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
exam <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")
```
:::


## 9.3.1 Plotting ridgeline graph: ggridges method

::: panel-tabset
## The Ex plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Ex Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = ENGLISH, 
           y = CLASS)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    bandwidth = 3.4,
    fill = lighten("#7097BB", .3),
    color = "white"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "English grades",
    expand = c(0, 0)
    ) +
  scale_y_discrete(name = NULL, expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 2.6))) +
  theme_ridges()
```
:::

:::

## Self Trying

::: panel-tabset
## The Plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = SCIENCE, 
           y = CLASS)) +
  geom_density_ridges(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    bandwidth = 3.4,
    fill = lighten("#7097BB", .3),
    color = "green"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "Science grades",
    expand = c(0, 0)
    ) +
  scale_y_discrete(name = NULL, expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 2.6))) +
  theme_ridges()
```
:::

:::

## 9.3.2 Varying fill colors along the x axis
::: panel-tabset
## The Ex plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Ex Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = ENGLISH, 
           y = CLASS,
           fill = stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Temp. [F]",
                       option = "C") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "English grades",
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(name = NULL, expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 2.6))) +
  theme_ridges()
```
:::

:::

```{.r .cell-code}

    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Temp. [F]",

  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "Math grades",
```

```{.r .cell-code}

       aes(x = MATHS, 
           y = CLASS,
           fill = stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,

  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Temp. [F]",
                       option = "C") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "Math grades",
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
```

## 9.3.3 Mapping the probabilities directly onto colour
::: panel-tabset
## The Ex plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam,
       aes(x = ENGLISH, 
           y = CLASS, 
           fill = 0.5 - abs(0.5-stat(ecdf)))) +
  stat_density_ridges(geom = "density_ridges_gradient", 
                      calc_ecdf = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Tail probability",
                       direction = -1) +
  theme_ridges()
```
:::

:::

## Self Trying
::: panel-tabset
## The plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-13-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam,
       aes(x = MATHS, 
           y = GENDER, 
           fill = 0.5 - abs(0.5-stat(ecdf)))) +
  stat_density_ridges(geom = "density_ridges_gradient", 
                      calc_ecdf = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Tail probability",
                       direction = -1) +
  theme_ridges()
```
:::

:::

## 9.3.4 Ridgeline plots with quantile lines
::: panel-tabset
## The Ex plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-15-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Ex Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam,
       aes(x = ENGLISH, 
           y = CLASS, 
           fill = factor(stat(quantile))
           )) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    geom = "density_ridges_gradient",
    calc_ecdf = TRUE, 
    quantiles = 4,
    quantile_lines = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "Quartiles") +
  theme_ridges()
```
:::

:::

## Self Trying
::: panel-tabset
## The plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-17-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam,
       aes(x = SCIENCE, 
           y = GENDER, 
           fill = factor(stat(quantile))
           )) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    geom = "density_ridges_gradient",
    calc_ecdf = TRUE, 
    quantiles = 4,
    quantile_lines = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "Quartiles") +
  theme_ridges()
```
:::

:::

:::panel-tabset
## Ex plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-19-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam,
       aes(x = ENGLISH, 
           y = CLASS, 
           fill = factor(stat(quantile))
           )) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    geom = "density_ridges_gradient",
    calc_ecdf = TRUE, 
    quantiles = c(0.025, 0.975)
    ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Probability",
    values = c("#FF0000A0", "#A0A0A0A0", "#0000FFA0"),
    labels = c("(0, 0.025]", "(0.025, 0.975]", "(0.975, 1]")
  ) +
  theme_ridges()
```
:::

:::

```{.r .cell-code}
           y = GENDER, 
           fill = factor(stat(quantile))
           )) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Probability",
    values = c("#FF0000A0", "#A0A0A0A0", "#0000FFA0"),
    labels = c("(0, 0.025]", "(0.025, 0.975]", "(0.975, 1]")
```

## 9.4.1 Plotting a Half Eye graph
::: panel-tabset
## The plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-23-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = RACE, 
           y = ENGLISH)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = 0.5,
               justification = -0.2,
               .width = 0,
               point_colour = NA)
```
:::

:::

## self-Trying
::: panel-tabset
## The plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-25-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = RACE, 
           y = SCIENCE)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = 0.5,
               justification = -0.2,
               .width = 0,
               point_colour = NA)
```
:::

:::

## 9.4.2 Adding the boxplot with geom_boxplot()
::: panel-tabset
## The Ex plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-27-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::

## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = RACE, 
           y = ENGLISH)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = 0.5,
               justification = -0.2,
               .width = 0,
               point_colour = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .20,
               outlier.shape = NA)
```
:::

:::

## 9.4.3 Adding the Dot Plots with stat_dots()
::: panel-tabset
## The Ex plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-31-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::

## The Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = RACE, 
           y = ENGLISH)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = 0.5,
               justification = -0.2,
               .width = 0,
               point_colour = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .20,
               outlier.shape = NA) +
  stat_dots(side = "left", 
            justification = 1.2, 
            binwidth = .5,
            dotsize = 2)
```
:::

:::

```{.r .cell-code}
           y = MATHS)) +

               justification = -0.2,
               .width = 0,
               point_colour = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .20,
               outlier.shape = NA) +
```

## 9.4.4 Finishing touch
::: panel-tabset
## Ex plot

::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](Hands_on_Ex0401_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-35-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## Ex Code

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(exam, 
       aes(x = RACE, 
           y = ENGLISH)) +
  stat_halfeye(adjust = 0.5,
               justification = -0.2,
               .width = 0,
               point_colour = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = .20,
               outlier.shape = NA) +
  stat_dots(side = "left", 
            justification = 1.2, 
            binwidth = .5,
            dotsize = 1.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_economist()
```
:::

:::
